stem: A product enters the manufacturing pipeline. The probability of passing raw material inspection is between 73% and 85% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing machining is 89%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing assembly is 87%. What is the overall probability that the product is shipped?

ground_truth.answer: P(shipped) ranges from 0.565 to 0.658 as the raw material inspection pass rate varies from 73% to 85%. Point estimate: 0.612.

solution_steps: The product must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the raw material inspection stage creates a 9.3 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the raw material inspection rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
